And when I saw you cooking tusok-tusok last sem starter, hehe, you looked really cute there. I don’t admire you just for your looks, but for how you manage your academic life here in [CALABARZON]. Being part of the student council is already tiring, but imagine being the president of Teatro de Liseo and consistently achieving high honors. I just know you must be tired from everything, but I hope you know that someone out here is rooting for you to overcome whatever life throws at you. You’re not just a crush for me—you’re a very special person. I include you in my prayers and wishes every day, hoping you still find a reason to smile even on your worst days. You are a very handsome man—not just a boy, a man. And if you think you’re not, I’m here to prove you wrong. For me, it’s not just about looks; it’s about how you treat others. At first, I was scared of you, especially during the election period, but as I got to know you more, I realized you were just a person too. If you’re wondering who I am, don’t bother. I’ll just be here, admiring you from afar as you slowly reach your dreams and finish your senior high school years. Don’t worry—I’ll still include you in my prayers even after you graduate. Keep going, my future MD :))

Just want to say that I really hate those people who are social climbers, trying hard, people pleasers and manipulative as fuck. Like I get it, we all want to be seen and heard but it is not really good when you start overstepping people’s boundaries and make it look like they’re responsible for what they’re going through. Always the paawa effect and using their mental conditions as an excuse as if they’re the only ones struggling when in fact others are also going through something. What bothers me as well is their behavior towards certain people cause what do you mean your nice and all when in front of them but talk shit like they’re “[REDACTED_NAME]” “cheaters” behind their back when in reality your just doing the things that your saying to others. What is the point of saying sorry countless times when people never learn and just keeps on doing the same mistake over and over again. It is right to always be kind to other people because you might not know what they are carrying but do remember that too much kindness can kill you nor pull you down. Always protect your inner peace even if it means neglecting something or someone. Never settle for these kinds of people cause you have nothing to do with their problems least of all their past traumas. It is not wrong to help them but they should first realize that change will only start among themselves, and if their not capable of improving amidst other people’s help, then that is their problem to take. [REDACTED_NAME] this message is an eye opener specially to those having a hard time with handling these kinds of people. You’re not wrong for feeling guilty, but brave to try and help someone. - A senior who saw a lot of shit already

| THIS IS ILLEGAL and any buyer might theoretically be seen as "aiding or abetting" a cybercrime or intellectual property infringement. Posting Ads and Selling and even allowing it more than once ...is not consistent with the objective of this freedom wall. Please protect the users here from possible scam or legal actions. The buyer primarily faces the risk of losing their money and access to the service, while the seller faces substantial fines and potential imprisonment The seller ("kimmoysz") is likely violating several Philippine laws: Republic Act No. (Intellectual Property Code of the Philippines): This is the most direct violation. The service providers (Netflix, YouTube) have exclusive rights to distribute access to their content. Reselling accounts without their authorization is a form of copyright or intellectual property infringement. Penalties for Seller: Imprisonment from 1 to 9 years and a fine of PHP 50,000 to PHP 1,500,000, along with potential civil damages and seizure of infringing materials. Republic Act No. (Cybercrime Prevention Act of ): This law penalizes "computer-related identity theft" and "illegal access". If the seller gained access to accounts through hacking or using compromised data (a common practice with cheap resold accounts), these provisions are violated. The law also increases penalties for crimes under the Revised Penal Code (like fraud or swindling) when committed using a computer system Republic Act No. (Electronic Commerce Act of ): This law prescribes penalties for piracy of protected materials through telecommunication networks and hacking. Republic Act No. (Internet Transactions Act of ): This law requires online merchants to be transparent, registered, and prohibits deceptive sales acts. The [DEPARTMENT](DTI) can issue takedown orders and impose fines on non-compliant or fraudulent sellers and platforms. Penalties for Seller: Fines ranging from PHP 20,000 to PHP 1,000,000 depending on the number of offenses, in addition to penalties from other laws. Significant Risks and Penalties for the Buyer A buyer assumes substantial risks and faces immediate, practical penalties that should not be underestimated. The consequences of buying these accounts are severe and guaranteed Immediate Account Termination: The primary and most common consequence is the sudden and permanent suspension of the service account by the provider (Netflix, YouTube) for a clear violation of their Terms of Service. The buyer immediately loses all access. Total Financial Loss: The buyer will almost certainly lose all money spent on the account. They have virtually no legal recourse to recover funds from a potentially fraudulent or untraceable seller. Your classmates survive by actually *PUTTING THE EFFORT* to study for that specific exam. By cheating, it shows you don’t have the effort to study at all. .-.. .. -- .- I wanna speak up for my cousin. To you cheater, you didn’t just hurt someone you shattered something sacred, trust. You were given love, loyalty, and honesty, and you chose to answer them with lies and betrayal. You made someone doubt their worth, question their value, and lose faith in what they once believed was real. The most painful part is that the person you hurt would have done anything for you, she believed in you even when she shouldn’t have. Cheating isn’t a mistake, it’s a choice one made with the full knowledge that it could destroy the person who loved you the most. And no matter how sorry you claim to be, the damage remains. The funny thing is, you never even said sorry to her. You don’t just break hearts, you change people. How can you preach about faith and peace when you built your happiness on someone else’s pain and trauma? You watched someone betray love and called it normal because you’ve done the same. You helped turn betrayal into a habit and truth into a joke. Maybe you think you were being loyal to a friend, but real loyalty isn’t about covering for someone’s wrongdoings, it’s about helping them become better. And by choosing silence, excuses, and denial, you didn’t just fail the person they hurt, you failed them too. By excusing what’s wrong, you didn’t just enable the cheater, you helped destroy someone’s faith in love, trust, and decency.

This entire situation with the [REDACTED_NAME] platform is creating extreme frustration and stress. The technical failures are draining, the notoriously slow loading times transform a compulsory requirement into a desperate race against the clock, where even starting the work weeks in advance offers no guarantee of progress. It is genuinely disheartening to be forced to accept substandard grades because a broken system refuses to load, a crucial factor completely beyond student control, despite trying and failing on every troubleshooting step. This persistent technical barrier shifts the focus entirely away from actual learning and toward the obsessive dread of meeting a rigid deadline. Adding to this problem is the significant content mismatch, while f2f classes may discuss specific humanities topics, the Wadhwani modules demand focus on distinct professional skills like communication, professionalism, problem-solving, and customer centricity. Though the platform supplies video content, the relevance of much of this material to the core curriculum of many programs is highly questionable. Furthermore, the agonizingly slow loading process frequently results in an error message after a wait of more than thirty minutes, particularly when using a mobile phone, which is the only computing device available to many students. While the speed is better on a computer, it remains unacceptably slow even with a fast internet connection and capable hardware. Since not all students own a personal laptop or have the necessary time to utilize campus library computers, the platform’s poor mobile performance directly penalizes students with limited financial resources and time constraints. I am not simply venting a complaint, I am questioning the facts —the efficacy, accessibility, and direct curricular relevance— of a compulsory educational tool, and I sincerely hope these critical issues can be formally addressed by the institution.

so i’ve been seeing wlw posts na nagcoconfess sakanilang mga crushes. might as well do an entry haha back in first year, i became friends with someone who unexpectedly changed how i understood love. for context, i’ve always been gay, and i usually find it easier to talk to guys than girls. but this girl—she was different. not loud, not the type who needed attention, yet somehow, she became the light i kept looking for. we got along easily. shared jokes, random talks, moments that felt a little too genuine to ignore. i’d catch myself smiling at small things she said, and yeah, maybe i read too much into them. she told me she was straight, and i respected that. but sometimes, her actions felt a bit different—confusing, even. maybe that’s just how she was. i did things just to be noticed, subtle stuff i wouldn’t normally do. not for attention, just... hope, maybe. but she never saw it that way. we eventually drifted apart, no big fight, just life doing its thing. we’re still friends, but she never knew i liked her. looking back, it felt real and simple—nothing exaggerated, just a part of growing up. i’m not with anyone right now, but there’s someone i genuinely care about. it’s not like i’m being unfaithful or anything, but sometimes she still slips into my thoughts—sudden, uninvited, yet oddly familiar. no nostalgia for what could’ve been. i’m past that part. it’s more about missing the vibe, the simple moments that made first year feel lighter. she somehow made school something i actually looked forward to, which is rare for me. and if she ever reads this, she’ll know... you’ll know. Nagmarathon na ako ng pagscroll dito sa fw and i saw a post by a maritime student from a while back. It was them being concerned about how overly strict the people in charge were there, or something of that sort. my main concern has nothing to do with the post itself, but the comments. just because a guy is concerned about matters of such, [REDACTED_NAME] "bading"? (I see those two comments. Two people actually said that.) And if he was, so what? And if he wasn't, so what too? Voicing out his concerns was not "weakness." It doesn't make him any less of a man. And to those who commented "bading," matutong rumespeto. If he's hesitant to pursue that course in college because of that experience, then let him be. To whoever posted that, my heart is with you. May you pursue a course and a job that you feel is safe and comfortable for you, without judgemental people screaming into your ear, telling you what to do with your life.

since i never got the chance to confess, i’m writing it all here. i just want to let you all know i’m not straight, i’m bisexual. it took me a while to accept that part of myself, but knowing her. she made me feel seen, understood, and accepted for who i truly am. you made me feel safe to be myself, without judgment, and that meant everything. we only became friends last school year, our first year here in [REDACTED_NAME]. i never expected that a simple friendship could mean so much. i never thought i’d fall for that smile and those eyes. you’ve always been beautiful, but i never wanted to risk our friendship because of what i felt. the more i got to know you, the more i realized it wasn’t just about your looks. it was about you. your passion, your drive, your heart. i admired your love for dancing. how you’d still show up after trainings even when you were tired or stressed. the way your eyes light up when you dance, it all showed how much dedication you have. it made me fall even deeper. i used to joke around and tease you, calling you my “crush,” never realizing that somewhere along the way, those jokes turned real. i started falling, not just for your beauty, but for your laugh, your humor, the way you’d tease back and make my simple days have some kilig. and what i admired most was how you still showed up even when you were going through something. you’d smile, dance, and laugh with our friend group, hiding the weight you carried so well. that silent strength, that smile you bring even when your world feels heavy. but it’s too late now, you’re already with someone else. and i respect that i could never bring myself to interfere or make things complicated. i’d rather stay silent than risk crossing a line or losing the friendship we have.i never got the chance to ask if there could’ve been something between us. then i realized, it wasn’t my place to ask. you’ve always felt out of my reach. [REDACTED_NAME], at ako ay lupa. just one of the many who admire you from afar. still, how could i not fall for someone like you? how could i not fall for the way you made me feel accepted, understood, and free to be me? if only i had the chance, i would’ve given you what you deserve. always admiring you silently, my crush, A.

To be educated enough to know you’re in a bad place, struggling, just wanting to be heard and to ask for help, it’s no longer seen as an act of bravery but as a sign of weakness and irritability. “Spoon-fed,” they say. “Super lucky niyo daw.” And people keep reminding us, why should we feel mentally unwell? Where is our right? Society constantly reminds us that we have no right to be miserable or depressed because to them, it’s only for attention and clout. To live every day where you are constantly trying and constantly showing up. But to live in an environment where your voice isn’t relevant, where you’re forced into the shadows, where your ideas don’t matter because you’re not smart enough. To go to school and come home with no one to talk to, not even your parents. You know what hurts the most? When people purposely skip your name and exclude you because you’ve made mistakes, caused damage, and they remind you that you’re too much. And yes, even when you’re physically sick and have no help from others, you’re still expected to be fine, to have no right not to be okay.

[REDACTED_NAME]. For a nursing student, cheating in exams is wrong not just for the usual reasons (academic integrity, fairness), but because it directly undermines the core principles and safety requirements of the nursing profession. Patient safety is at stake. Exams are designed to ensure you have the foundational knowledge required to make safe, life-altering decisions. Cheating means you haven't truly mastered that material. In a clinical setting, this knowledge gap could lead to a medication error, a wrong dosage calculation, or a failure to recognize a critical change in a patient's condition. Unlike many other professions, a nurse's mistake due to lack of knowledge can directly, immediately, and severely harm or kill a patient. Your education is literally preparing you for life-or-death situations. As a future nurse, you are accountable for all your actions and omissions. This means taking full responsibility for your competence and decisions. Cheating shows a lack of willingness to be accountable for your own learning and performance. Nurses must be honest about mistakes, documentation, and patient care. If you are willing to be dishonest in school, it raises a serious question about whether you will be honest when you inevitably face a difficult situation or error in practice. In short, a nursing student who cheats is not just cheating on a test; they are cheating their future patients out of the competent, safe, and ethical care they deserve.

Those five students used their phones not only for themselves but also to help the section like, what the heck. They didn’t cheat to be better than you guys, they did it to pass bcs maybe they are “[REDACTED_NAME]” nga and they cant make sabayan with you guys. Still, I know it’s wrong. I honestly dont hate you guys, even if it feels unfair to those of us who really studied. bcs I also know that some of you have personal reasons, and I won’t judge you for doing that unless you keep repeating it. Hey everyone, daily reminder for us to be kind to anyone! 💖 It’s all our first time living this life, and none of us are perfect. Some days just don’t go our way and that doesn’t give us the right to judge other people. That goes for our professors, the staff, our classmates, and everyone else who’s just trying to get through life. I’ve seen some posts talking negatively about certain programs and professors, and honestly, this isn’t the right platform for that. Yes, we all have freedom of speech, but please use it with respect. Freedom doesn’t mean stepping on someone else. We don’t know people’s life or what they are going through, so let’s not act like we do. Also, just because something doesn’t work for you doesn’t mean it won’t work for everyone. We all have our own ways of handling things and surviving college life. Instead of spreading negativity, let’s take action and lift each other up because we are here for something greater. Our professors are doing their best—they show up, they teach, and they’re helping us get the education we deserve. So yeah... please, just be kind. It doesn’t cost anything. ✨💊

I was once a suicid*l person that was back in . When I was 21, I was diagnosed with bipolar disorder. It’s not something I usually talk about. After all, who would truly understand? There are days when I feel so happy. too happy, even that I can’t contain it. I laugh, I talk endlessly, I become the life of the crowd. But there are also moments when my emotions turn dark, when I become easily irritated, aggressive, and say things that hurt others without meaning to. My friends, thankfully, already know what’s going on with me. Especially now. Lately, I’ve been so tired and overwhelmed by the never-ending workload and pressure from school. As an officer, people expect me to always be strong, kind, and dependable, easy to say a “role model.” But sometimes, that label feels like a heavy chain around my neck. One night, while studying for my upcoming midterm exams, I broke down. I couldn’t hold it in anymore. I wanted to cry, to scream, to let it all out. I felt like I was drowning in my own thoughts again. And yes, I tried to hurt myself. I wanted to see the blood, as if letting it out could somehow release the pain inside me. I cried until I couldn’t anymore. I wasn’t just crying because of school or grades but I was crying because of the weight of expectations, of always having to be “enough” in the eyes of everyone else. People always say, “You have to be kind.” But are they kind to me? Some instructors forget that students are human too. We all get tired. We all have limits. Sometimes I wonder, if I were to die by suic*de because of the things said to me, because of the pressure this system puts on us , would it finally make them realize something? Would they finally understand that we need rest too? Everyone has their own way of coping with stress. But what about people like me? the ones who are clinically diagnosed, the ones some call “mentally ill”? We don’t need pity all we need understanding. I’m tired. I feel lost. I don’t know what to do or where to seek help. Sometimes I read other people’s stories online words full of pain and desperation and I realize I’m not alone. We are all screaming silently, hoping someone will finally listen. I just hope that one day, this pressure will end. That someday, we can breathe without guilt. That people will see that being kind should go both ways.

Hey everyone, With midterms just around the corner, I think it's important we all fake a moment to reflect on how we approach this. We've all been working hard-sacrificing sleep, juggling responsibilities, and doing our best to prepare. That effort deserves to be respected, not just by ourselves, but by each other too. Now, I know there were some things that happened during the prelims-like phone use during exams-that may have gone unnoticed because the CCTV didn't catch it. But just because no one was called out doesn't mean it was okay. Let's be real: we all know what's fair and what's not. This isn't about pointing fingers. It's about making sure we're all on the same page. Cheating, in any form, doesn't just hurt the person doing it-it undermines the whole group. It makes the effort of those who studied honestly feel like it doesn't matter. And that's not the kind of environment we want to create.

To: [CALABARZON]-B Management, CIs, [REDACTED_NAME], [REDACTED_NAME] I want to shine a light on some issues happening in the Nursing Department because we care and we want our voices heard. What we’re seeing: 🌷Students in the Nursing program feel like we’re not always prioritized from scheduling of clinicals, to lab access, to support when things get overwhelming. 🌷The workload and pressure are huge, and sometimes it feels like resources labs, simulations, clinical placements or faculty support aren’t keeping up. 🌷Mental health, our well-being? It’s often pushed aside while we chase everything else. 🌷We love that the Nursing program aims to be “transformative, globally competitive”. But when the day-to-day struggles pile up, it’s hard to fully feel that promise. What we’re asking you to do: 🌷Please give us more transparent communication what’s the plan for lab use, clinical schedules, extra support sessions? Let us know ahead of time. 🌷Increase mental health & student-well-being support specifically for the Nursing students workshops, check-ins, peer groups like the THK. 🌷Ensure resources equipment, simulation labs, enough faculty are up to standard and meet the program’s high aim. Let student feedback be a meaningful part of decisions in the Nursing Dept so we’re not just heard but included. And please prioritize us not just in words, but in actions: better scheduling, fairer workload, and actual follow through. We believe in [CALABARZON]-B and the Nursing program. We know the potential is there. But we also believe that for us to shine and succeed, we need support, care, and prioritization from you. Love you, Nursing!

Reply to Upon seeing the online results for the World Scholars Cup, I've noticed something concerning. The prevalence of competitors seemingly almost exclusively from Vietnam, combined with the "Tournament of Champions" at Yale, raises a significant question: Is this competition truly about academic excellence, or is it more a matter of who can afford to travel? I believe this event, while perhaps prestigious on paper, may be prioritizing a select few rather than benefiting the majority of the student body. As an ordinary student, my perspective is that the department's focus should shift. The school should choose activities and events that genuinely benefit the welfare of the student majority over those that merely bring "prestige" (especially if that prestige is questionable). Being an ORDINARY STUDENT WHO IS TOLERATED (because the favorites are always treated well), I've observed many instances where core needs in the school have been avoided or disregarded in favor of what appears to be a misplaced priority. This brings us to a critical financial issue. I heared from another class that a teacher openly reacted to a previous post here in front of the class, admitting the truth of the school's constant refrain: "We lack funds." If this is the case—and a teacher's admission makes it seem quite public—then the school must address this budget paradox: We lack funds, but we allocate budget for non-necessities. We lack funds, but we neglect the welfare of the majority. We lack funds, but we mismanage school activities. It is disheartening when the financial challenges we face are so obvious, yet resources are channeled toward activities that benefit a tiny fraction of the students. My hope is that the school will urgently re-focus on what should be done first—the foundational needs of the student body—and genuinely consider what is beneficial for everyone, not just what looks good on a press release.

As a student leader, I would like to share my thoughts objectively. From what I have observed, there has been no mismanagement on the part of our High School administration, this is only my opinion no bashing. When many teachers and students got sick, our high school management immediately shifted to online synchronous or online classes a very very very quick and responsible decision that prioritized everyone’s well-being. Inannounce agad, while college are waiting for online sa HS online na agad tayo. I am not saying this out of emotion, but out of facts and firsthand experiences. Our principal is in school as early and often stays until 6 or 7pm we personally she her go out of her office. My classmates and I personally see her every day at the hallways. Of course, we don’t want to romanticize her overwork, but we must also give credit where it is due. She can only do so much [REDACTED_NAME] our teachers and advisers also share responsibility, especially in guiding us during homeroom time. Speaking of which, our adviser have been doing an excellent job. We truly appreciate how he handles our HG classes and support us in different activities. He makes sure to motivate us just like our own dad. He gives his heart just to support us in everything we do and makes sure the homeroom time is utilized. I hope the others get a supportive adviser like ours. Yes there are teachers who might not be good [REDACTED_NAME] but the teachers who are performing madami sila like [REDACTED_NAME] Sir [REDACTED_NAME], Sir [REDACTED_NAME], Ms. [REDACTED_NAME], Sir [REDACTED_NAME] they are really really good po Regarding Teacher’s Day naman [REDACTED_NAME], may we also remember that it’s a day meant to honor our teachers [REDACTED_NAME]. Let’s celebrate it with gratitude and respect instead of unnecessary negativity. Dami nating negativity for a day were the Teacher's themselves enjoyed. And if there are concerns, please go directly to the Principal or your Area Chair or your advisers they have always kept an open door. I’ve never seen such accessibility and care from school teachers during my grade school years. That’s something special about [CALABARZON] high the family cares. As for budget allocations, there are proper procedures and people that handle these matters. If you have questions, the best way is to ask the LSSG siguro or the proper offices, and verify facts first before making assumptions. Let’s also acknowledge the efforts of the coaches who tirelessly coach students in competitions from Journalism, quiz bees, impromptus, orations, to the World Scholars Cup. Their guidance comes from years and years of experience and wisdom that self study alone cannot replace. And of course in any competition self study should really be done by all contestants and not just relying on time given. The principal has even been mentors of my older cousins in dspc, rspc and they said she is a really great but can be very strict coach. I’ve been part of [CALABARZON] po since Grade 7, and it honestly pains me to read baseless criticisms, especially from those who might be new or transferrees and haven’t yet seen how much work and care go into everything Finally regarding the World Scholars Cup yes, anyone can win medals po, just like in any competition. But those medals are earned through hard work, preparation, and merit. Instead of pulling others down, let’s reflect and ask ourselves: What can I do to improve? Complaining won’t take us far. Hindi [REDACTED_NAME] rank 1-3 awarding [REDACTED_NAME] local school competition na 3 lang ang available na medals since hundred and thousands join it. International meaning from all over the world po. Let’s base our opinions and all our posting on facts, not rumors and not envy [REDACTED_NAME]. A short academic or mental health break would greatly help us regain balance, improve our performance, and maintain a healthy mindset.

this isn’t meant as criticism toward our university, but as a sincere reminder to listen and care a little more. i’m an [CALABARZON] student, and lately, i’ve noticed how many of my classmates and fellow students are coughing, sniffling, and clearly not feeling well. whether it’s inside the classroom, in the van, or at the dorms, it’s worrying to see how common it has become. i know the university has advised us not to fear the influenza outbreak, to wear face masks, and to stay home when we’re sick. but the truth is, many of us hesitate to be absent because we’re afraid of missing lessons and falling behind. it’s not that we don’t care about our health, we just feel pressured to keep going even when our bodies are asking us to rest. i’m speaking on behalf of students who might not have the courage to say this out loud. we’re not being irresponsible, we’re trying our best to balance our studies and our well-being. all we’re hoping for is a little more understanding and flexibility, especially for those who are struggling to recover. please listen not only to our words but to our silence and exhaustion. health should never come second to academics. we love [CALABARZON], and we’re proud to be part of it, but we also hope to feel that same care and concern from our university, because sometimes, compassion is the lesson that matters most.

Beyond failing to grasp the concerns of their own constituents, the LNSC can’t even articulate something as basic as the fee for transcripts. Why not break it down plainly? They released a memorandum claiming the membership fee is “voluntary,” yet anyone who doesn’t pay risks being marked absent. Voluntary...but with consequences? Very innovative. Let’s be realistic! No student would actually want to miss those events. Many are second-guessing the ₱550 not out of apathy, but probably because of financial limits, conflicting responsibilities, personal priorities, and other valid constraints that were conveniently overlooked. Not everyone has a wallet sponsored by blind optimism.😜 And frankly, isn’t it long overdue for LNSC to address these mounting issues? The membership fee is just one of several concerns piling up. If they genuinely care about the very people who elected them, why is there no reciprocity in representation? They were remarkably visible during the campaign period, and now they’re as vocal as a microphone on mute at a leadership forum. It’s almost poetic how fast “for the students” turns into “kindly refer to the memo.” 😬 If their energy post-election were half as alive as their campaign smiles, student concerns wouldn’t be collecting dust like unclaimed receipts. Reply to While I understand and sympathize with you regarding the mismanagement of events, funds, and lectures, I don’t think it’s fair to direct that frustration towards the delegates of the World Scholar’s Cup. You said you did your research and mentioned that even competitors who place around 200th could still earn medals. And though that may be true, it overlooks the actual scale of the competition. The World Scholar’s Cup involves thousands of scholars from across the globe. Naturally, a wider pool of participants means a larger number of awardees. Take for example the International Vedic Mathematics Olympiad where the Philippines brought home home 244 Gold, 488 Silver, and 746 Bronze medals. Seems quite a lot, doesn’t it? That’s just how it really is in global academic competitions. Either way, representing the country on a global stage is already something to be proud of. I believe there’s much honor and prestige in that alone. You diminsh the excellence of our students by saying “anyone could win a medal” which contradicts your earlier statement saying you don’t mean to invalidate their talents. Just my two cents. We can criticize how the department manages its operations without dragging down the students who are doing their best despite those shortcomings.

Reply to It’s the same here in college. There’s a clear lack of support and appreciation for professors. Just look at the last Teacher’s Day which only LIMA, I think, organized something for them. Classes still go on even with influenza-like illnesses spreading. Even teachers who are sick still attend classes because they have to -otherwise, they won’t get paid. Imagine that. The system leaves them with no real choice but to keep going, even at the expense of their health. Perhaphs some of them are just caught up in a flawed system. Where were they then? Overseas? They’ve been planning a lot of things, but most of those plans never get executed properly examples are the Foundation Day celebration and the midterm exam schedule. The exam that was first set on October 31 and then suddenly moved to October 25, causing confusion among both students and faculty. Did they only realize too late that October 31 isn’t a feasible date for midterms? And what’s more ironic is that students are being taught to voice out their concerns, yet when they actually do, they’re silenced or ignored. Isn’t that exactly what’s needed to bring reform and real change—not just in school policies, but in the country as a whole?

Note: Please don’t take this as an attack I don’t usually rant here, but the situation with the CON department has just become too much. They keep asking so much from us, yet give back so little in return. For years, we’ve been paying the membership fee without knowing it wasn’t actually mandatory. It was never explained properly. And for years, during every Pistakasan, Nurses Week, or Scrubs Off, we’re stuck in our clinical rotations, only allowed to attend the opening, unless we’re players or lucky enough to have CIs who let us join the finals or closing. Even then, we still have to do case presentations inside the library. Despite how unfair this is, we stayed silent. We’ve also been asking for refunds for the laboratory fees (₱650 per semester) that we weren’t even able to use, fees that were required just so we could attend our duties. They even made us fill out a form detailing our out-of-pocket lab expenses, and we thought that meant they were finally addressing it. But nothing happened. No refund. Then there’s the hepa vaccination issue. It was offered through the department and required three doses, but even after a year, we still haven’t received all of them. When we followed up, we were told to just get them outside instead, still with no refund. We also filed a formal complaint about another issue, where we paid a huge amount for something that turned out to be a terrible experience, not at all worth the money. A higher-up told us that discussions were ongoing, but once again, nothing came out of it. No action, no response. Just empty promises. And honestly, I can’t help but question why the LNSC membership fee is as high as ₱550, adding up to over half a million for the whole CON department, when we’re already paying so much for tuition, miscellaneous, and other fees. They’ll say the membership is optional, but not paying means you get marked absent, and one absence equals three make-up duties or classes, which also cost a lot. So in reality, it’s not optional at all. I’m just so frustrated. It feels like all we do is pay, pay, and pay, without ever getting our money’s worth. I feel sad for my parents who work so hard for this. Not everyone is privileged enough to shoulder all these extra costs. And on top of everything, we still have to pay for our affiliation fee (I have nothing against this, I’m just frustrated with the line of fees I have to pay)

Dear [CALABARZON], I hope you take a moment to understand where we are all coming from. We know that no one wants to get infected by the virus that’s currently spreading, and we truly understand your side too. But please, be considerate of the students. Many of us are anxious and trying our best to stay healthy while keeping up with our responsibilities. It would mean so much if classes could be made asynchronous for the meantime. Some of us, especially dormers, are more exposed and vulnerable, and getting sick is not something easy to go through, physically, mentally, and financially. We care deeply about our health and the people around us, and we hope that you care just as much. We’re not asking for much, only for a bit of understanding and compassion during this time. Please hear us out students, we just want to stay safe while continuing to learn.

With the school showing so much negligence, I can’t stay silent anymore. I’ve seen several posts here on the freedom wall, and I completely agree with them. I’m a freshman too, and I honestly can’t believe that the school is still holding events and activities while there are actual emergency situations happening right now. Seriously, what are the school’s values even for? They keep saying they care for students’ health and safety, but their actions show the opposite. If all they can do is ignore what’s happening around us, collect tuition fees, and keep piling up workloads, then what are we even paying for? Is our tuition really worth it if the school can’t even prioritize our well-being? It’s disappointing to see a university that claims to care, yet turns a blind eye when students need understanding and protection the most.

I would like to respectfully express my thoughts about [REDACTED_NAME]’s decision not to suspend classes despite the current situation. It may seem to the administration that students are just being lazy, but we are genuinely concerned for our safety with earthquakes happening and illnesses spreading everywhere. During our orientation, we were repeatedly told that the university cares deeply about students’ health and well-being. They even highlighted having a consultation room as proof of that care. But now, it is becoming clear that maybe that consultation room exists because of the school’s own negligence toward its students’ health. No health break, no suspension, no real concern. Were all those long messages about caring for students just for show? As a freshman, I truly believed this school valued its students more than profit. But seeing how this situation is being handled makes me question that belief. Other universities like [NCR] and [CALABARZON] Manila have already suspended their classes. Why can’t ours do the same? At this point, what we are asking for is not unreasonable. We are not asking to stop classes, only to continue them online for our safety. With Taal Volcano already on Alert Level 1 and earthquakes happening, shouldn’t students be with their families, preparing in case things worsen? Education matters, but so does safety. I hope the school remembers that before something unfortunate happens.

Despite everything, she’s my ray of hope, my best friend, and my future. She’s always there—smiling every day, running toward me like a child eager to share every thought in her head. When I’m feeling down, she never fails to make me laugh. She tells me she feels sad when I’m sad, and in that moment, my worries fade away. Thinking about it, she’s the reason I couldn’t stay the same person I once was. I want her to have everything—the world itself. To turn that dream into reality, I’ve kept studying and listening diligently, because I don’t want her to live an average life. I wish for her happiness, and to make her happy, I knew I had to change. One day, I’ll take you to Switzerland. I promise.

I’d like to share one of the best moments I could ever recall when I was at riverside. I remember I was almost late for school, and I was still highschool so I stopped at riverside it was way back December 14, , while crossing I noticed a student as well with her headphones on and I saw a crazy truck speeding on a school zone like really? So without a second thought I stopped the girl from walking across the street and saved her life before the truck hits her because i know if I wasn’t there I don’t know what would’ve happened. It just feels like I was late to school for some reason and it was perfect timing that I was there to save her on time. I’ve noticed a lot of negative feed on this freedom wall so I decided to maybe change the tone a bit and share one of the heroic things I did. And hopefully this story also helps remind others that there’s still hope, goodness, and purity in this school and always choose to do good because it will also help you to inspire others to do right even when no one’s looking.

“A strong secure leader accepts blame and gives credit. A weak insecure leader gives blame and takes credit.” CAMP Council—seriously, do better. The way you handled this situation is not just disappointing, it is alarming. Turning serious matters into jokes is the kind of insensitivity that no leader should ever show. And yet no one is taking accountability. You are in the council for a reason, so act like it. Think before you post. We are not being killjoys. We are standing up for what is right. Values are not for display, they are meant to be practiced, and sadly you have failed to do so. This is not even the first time I have heard of issues about this year’s council. You still have time to fix your image, but right now the legacy you are building is far from admirable. CAMP Council was once known for excellence, but what we are seeing now is the opposite. What is the purpose of being a professor if you don’t teach? First of all, I want to say that it’s not easy being a student or a professor, but we all do what we have to do because it’s our responsibility. As students, we know we need to do what you ask us to do, and I understand that. Honestly, I don’t remember anything from your class. All I can remember is you bragging about your life. I know you have a lot of achievements, and I respect that. But as a professor, you have a responsibility to teach us the subject, not your life story. You have a great sense of humor, and I admire that. However, as a student, I haven’t learned anything about the actual subject matter. Another thing, please don’t downgrade or embarrass students just because they didn’t iron their uniforms. Yes, we are responsible for presenting ourselves professionally, but have you considered the student's situation? Have you asked if he or she lives nearby? Maybe they live in a dorm or an apartment and don’t have access to an iron. It would be more appropriate to address such matters privately, rather than pointing it out in front of everyone multiple times. We notice it too; you don’t need to keep repeating it. It's not the last time you're doing it; that's why I cannot be in silence anymore. I hope you get to read this, Doc. – THANK YOU SO MUCH, ADMINS

Maybe instead of mocking others, try understanding what they're actually going through. Compassion isn't a sign of weakness - but mocking struggling students definitely is. 1st year here. I'm kind of having a hard time adjusting to this school's way of teaching. I get that having quizzes will help us improve, but I'm not one of those gifted kids that can learn fast in a short time. "Time management is the key," it is not the case for everyone. Despite struggling to juggle these quizzes and activities, I keep telling myself that I'll be able to adjust sooner or later. Although it's only been 2 weeks into this school year, I already feel so tired. Even when I had a full rest, my body and mind just feels burned out. "The quizzes just keep coming one after another. Sometimes in one subject, we even have up to 5 quizzes in a single day. I hope it could be slowed down a bit, because it’s really tiring. It's too early to be crashing out over this. Uhm to the seniors who survived this kind of treatment, how do you manage time?
